arraySpec.description: Post-decode array/indexed-message semantics: one message id is sent repeatedly, each frame carrying an index signal plus per-element data, and the consumer reconstructs an array. This is a STORAGE/ingest hint layered on top of the wire layout — an array message will typically also use `multiplexing` to describe how the bits are packed; the two coexist. Index and element roles are DERIVED from this block (no redundant per-signal field).

indexSignal.description: Name of the signal in this message that carries the element index (e.g. `cell_index`). Must reference a signal in the same message.

size.description: Total element count (e.g. number of cells). Use `null` for a dynamic or unknown count.

elementSignals.description: Names of the per-element data signals — the values that repeat once per index (e.g. `cell_voltage`, `cell_temp`). Each must reference a signal in the same message and must not be the `indexSignal`.

storage.description: Typed hint that drives sink/ingest behavior when reconstructing the array. `series_per_index`: emit one independent time series per index value (e.g. cell_voltage[0], cell_voltage[1], …) — best for per-element trending. `array_column`: store each full frame's elements as a single array/vector column (one row per timestamp holding the whole vector) — best for snapshots you query as a unit. `snapshot`: keep only the latest fully-populated array, overwriting prior values — best for a current-state view with no history.

signalGroup.description: DBC SIG_GROUP_ metadata grouping signals within a message.

containedMessage.description: A sub-PDU inside a container message (ISO-TP / AUTOSAR PDU container). No nested containers.

environmentVariable.description: DBC EV_ environment variable on a network/bus.

message.description: A CAN(-FD) frame definition and its signals.

name.description: Message name, unique within its network.

frame_id.description: Arbitration/frame id. Max 0x1FFFFFFF (29-bit). Standard frames must be <= 0x7FF.

is_extended_frame.description: True for 29-bit extended identifiers, false for 11-bit standard.

length.description: Payload length (DLC) in bytes. Classic CAN <= 8; CAN FD <= 64.

send_type.description: Raw DBC GenMsgSendType; bus-load classification in contract §7.

cycle_time.description: Nominal transmit period in milliseconds. For cyclic messages this is the period used by the bus-load calculation (default 1000). For triggered messages it is optional and ignored by bus-load; null for purely event-driven messages.

computedSignals.description: Virtual signals derived from signals of this message.

network.description: A CAN/CAN-FD bus within a vehicle (one DBC ≈ one network).

id.description: Network id, unique within its vehicle; the namespace for its messages.

filename.description: Source filename when split into a per-network file, for round-tripping.

baudrate.description: Nominal/arbitration bitrate in bits per second.

fd_baudrate.description: CAN FD data-phase bitrate in bits per second; present ⇒ CAN FD.

samplePoint.description: Nominal-phase sample point as a percentage (e.g. 87.5).

valueTables.description: Network-local shared enumerations (resolved before project-shared tables).

computedSignals.description: Virtual signals derived from signals across messages on this network (cross-message expressions).

environment_variables.description: DBC EV_ environment variables on this bus.
